title: Make review-evidence check runs converge after cancellation
goal: Prevent GitHub concurrency cancellation from leaving an obsolete cancelled Review evidence gate check-run that blocks an otherwise green exact-head gate, while preserving fail-closed evidence binding, fork safety and bounded CI use.

acceptance.assertion: A deterministic workflow or integration fixture reproduces the PR #1441 state: a pull_request_target check-run is cancelled by concurrency, a later issue_comment evaluation publishes success for the same exact head, and GitHub can still report the PR blocked.
For every evaluated current PR head, the required Review evidence gate is represented by a terminal success or failure check-run/status that cannot be shadowed by an obsolete cancelled run.

The solution continues to execute only trusted main-branch governance code, never checks out PR code, keeps exact base/head/diff binding and retains trusted-association checks for native reviews and rechecks.
Fork and Dependabot PRs retain a documented maintainer-triggered recheck path without granting write tokens to untrusted review events or requiring manual reruns of stale workflow attempts.
Concurrency still suppresses redundant work, but cancellation cannot strand a required check; tests cover push/comment/review races, edited or deleted evidence and repeated rechecks on one head.
Unit and workflow-contract tests, GitHub CI, an exact-head review-evidence run and a live PR readback prove CLEAN merge state without operator rerunning a cancelled check-run.

metadata.observed_resolution: The cancelled PR-head run had to be rerun as attempt 2 before mergeStateStatus became CLEAN.
scope_exclusions: treating commit status success as sufficient when a blocking check-run remains
executing pull-request code in a privileged workflow
bypassing fork or Dependabot token restrictions
